description: When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1030, what caveat is documented for Central?

Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will be disconnected. This cycle of connecting and disconnecting continues until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To prevent this unnecessary reconnection, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

description: When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1030, what caveat is documented for Certificates?

Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

description: For Classifier policy modifications to be secure, Aruba strongly encourages modifications be done as a three-step process: Bring down the port, modify, and bring the port back up.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1030, what caveat is documented for Classifiers?

Feature Caveat: Classifiers - For Classifier policy modifications to be secure, Aruba strongly encourages modifications be done as a three-step process: Bring down the port, modify, and bring the port back up.

description: Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1030, what limitation is mentioned for Classifiers?
Feature Caveat: Classifiers - Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

description: No other checkpoint besides "startup-configuration" gets migrated during the upgrade process.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1030, what caveat is documented for CMF?
CMF - No other checkpoint besides 'startup-configuration' gets migrated during the upgrade process.

description: When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1030, what caveat is documented for Hot Patch?

Feature Caveat: Hot Patch - When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

feature: IGMP/PIM on 6-in-6, Loopback and GRE interfaces
description: IGMP cannot be enabled on either Loopback or GRE interfaces. IGMP and PIM is not supported on a 6-in-6 Tunnel.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1030, what caveat is documented for IGMP/PIM on 6-in-6, Loopback and GRE interfaces?
Feature Caveat: IGMP/PIM on 6-in-6, Loopback and GRE interfaces - IGMP cannot be enabled on either Loopback or GRE interfaces. IGMP and PIM are not supported on a 6-in-6 Tunnel.

description: Reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services within the system are not recommended to be used for other services. When two services use the same port there is chance of unexpected behaviors from these services. Best practice is to use unique port for each service across system.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1030, what caveat is documented for IP-SLA?

Feature Caveat: IP-SLA - Reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services within the system are not recommended to be used for other services. When two services use the same port there is a chance of unexpected behaviors from these services. Best practice is to use unique ports for each service across the system.

description: In an environment with devices running AOS-Switch, do not enable UDLD on the same link. The UDLD session can toggle between up and down continuously when both MACsec and UDLD is enabled on the same link.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1030, what caveat is documented for MACsec and UDLD?

Feature Caveat: MACsec and UDLD - In an environment with devices running AOS-Switch, do not enable UDLD on the same link. The UDLD session can toggle between up and down continuously when both MACsec and UDLD is enabled on the same link.

description: WAN MACsec with a custom ether type will not be established between CX devices when there are PVOS switches in the bypass.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1030, what caveat is documented for MACsec?
Feature Caveat: MACsec - WAN MACsec with a custom ether type will not be established between CX devices when there are PVOS switches in the bypass.

description: In an environment with a Cisco device, the Cisco device must be designated as the key server. Designating the AOS-CX as the key server results in complete traffic loss.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1030, what limitation is mentioned for MACsec?
Feature Caveat: MACsec - In an environment with a Cisco device, the Cisco device must be designated as the key server. Designating the AOS-CX as the key server results in complete traffic loss.

description: In an environment with Cisco and FlexFabric or H3C devices, do not update confidentiality-offset on the live channel. There can be complete traffic loss for an extended period on the MACsec channel when confidentiality-offset is updated on both ends.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1030, what additional caveat is documented for MACsec?

Feature Caveat: MACsec - In an environment with Cisco and FlexFabric or H3C devices, do not update confidentiality-offset on the live channel. There can be complete traffic loss for an extended period on the MACsec channel when confidentiality-offset is updated on both ends.

description: MACsec uses a software-based implementation to track start and stop times for secure channels and secure associations. As the implementation is software-based, the stop times for MACsec secure channel and secure associations are only updated when they are deleted and therefore never updated in the output of the show macsec status detailed command.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1030, what additional caveat is documented for MACsec?

Feature Caveat: MACsec - The implementation uses a software-based approach to track start and stop times for secure channels and associations. Consequently, the stop times are only updated when the secure channel or association is deleted, and thus are not reflected in the output of the show macsec status detailed command.

description: In an environment with Cisco devices, when the GCM-AES-XPN-128 or GCM-AES-XPN-256 cipher suite is used for establishing the MACsec channel, the MKA policy on the Cisco device must be configured with ssci-based-on-sci.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1030, what additional caveat is documented for MACsec?

Feature Caveat: MACsec - In an environment with Cisco devices, when the GCM-AES-XPN-128 or GCM-AES-XPN-256 cipher suite is used for establishing the MACsec channel, the MKA policy on the Cisco device must be configured with ssci-based-on-sci.

description: Multicast traffic cannot be run under GRE, 6in4, or 6in6 tunnels,

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1030, what caveat is documented for Multicast?
Multicast traffic cannot be run under GRE, 6in4, or 6in6 tunnels.

description: Priority-based flow control (PFC) is not supported on a split port.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1030, what caveat is documented for PFC?
Feature Caveat: PFC - Priority-based flow control (PFC) is not supported on a split port.

description: Pim Active-Active is not supported on overlay VXLAN SVIs.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1030, what caveat is documented for PIM-SM?
Feature Caveat: PIM-SM - Pim Active-Active is not supported on overlay VXLAN SVIs.

description: REST supports the 'admin' and 'operator' roles but does not work with TACACS+ command authorization.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1030, what caveat is documented for REST?
REST supports the 'admin' and 'operator' roles but does not work with TACACS+ command authorization.

description: When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1030, what is the caveat related to SNMP?

Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

description: Issuing the traceroute command with the ip-option loosesourceroute parameter fails inan overlay EVPN-VxLAN deployment.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1030, what caveat is documented for Traceroute?
Feature Caveat: Traceroute - Issuing the traceroute command with the ip-option loosesourceroute parameter fails in an overlay EVPN-VxLAN deployment.

description: Traceroute v4/v6 over VXLAN fails to find intermediate next-hop IP information from a source VTEP in Virtual Active Gateway environment (the SVI is the same as theActive Gateway IP).

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1030, what limitation is mentioned for Traceroute?

Feature Caveat: Traceroute - Traceroute v4/v6 over VXLAN fails to find intermediate next-hop IP information from a source VTEP in Virtual Active Gateway environment (the SVI is the same as the Active Gateway IP).

description: Symptom: Incorrect sysDescr displayed in snmpwalk/get output. Scenario: When a non-default system description is configured and then reverted to the default, performing an SNMP walk or get operation may display the wrong sysDescr. Workaround: The System Description can still be viewed using the CLI.

scenario: When a non-default system description is configured and then reverted to the default, performing an SNMP walk or get operation may display the wrong sysDescr.

description: Symptom: When DHCP snooping option 82 behavior needed but not supported on 9300. Scenario: DHCP client is trying to get an IP and DHCP server is configured to give IPv4 address based on the Circuit-id and Remote-Id. Workaround: Enable the dhcp-relay option 82 circuit-id port-id command to achieve DHCP Snooping option 82 behavior in DHCP-Relay itself. By enabling this command, DHCP Relay will send option 82 information similar to DHCP-Snooping.
symptom: When DHCP snooping option 82 behavior needed but not supported on 9300.
scenario: DHCP client is trying to get an IP and DHCP server is configured to give IPv4 address based on the Circuit-id and Remote-Id.
workaround: Enable the dhcp-relay option 82 circuit-id port-id command to achieve DHCP Snooping option 82 behavior in DHCP-Relay itself. By enabling this command, DHCP Relay will send option 82 information similar to DHCP-Snooping.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.14.1030, what issue was resolved in DHCP Relay Bug 336714?

DHCP Relay (Bug ID 336714): Symptom: When DHCP snooping option 82 behavior is needed but not supported on 9300. Scenario: A DHCP client attempts to get an IP address, while the DHCP server is configured to provide IPv4 addresses based on Circuit-id and Remote-Id. Workaround: Enable the dhcp-relay option 82 circuit-id port-id command to achieve DHCP Snooping option 82 behavior in DHCP-Relay itself. By enabling this command, DHCP Relay will send option 82 information similar to DHCP-Snooping.

description: Symptom: BGP neighbor session is stuck in the connect state. Scenario: The BGP neighbor password was changed for the respective switches using a non-CLI method. Subsequently, a switch reboot was performed on only one side of the switch pair. Workaround: On the non-upgraded platform, it is necessary to run the clear bgp command.
symptom: BGP neighbor session is stuck in the connect state.
scenario: The BGP neighbor password was changed for the respective switches using a non-CLI method. Subsequently, a switch reboot was performed on only one side of the switch pair.
workaround: On the non-upgraded platform, it is necessary to run the clear bgp command.

description: Symptom: Client ARP entries are not aging out on the VSX peer switch. Scenario: When an ARP entry is learned and synced to the VSX peer, followed by a client IP change at the end host, the old ARP entries do not age out on the switch. Workaround: Manually clear the ARP entries on the respective VSX peer switch.
symptom: Client ARP entries are not aging out on the VSX peer switch.
scenario: When an ARP entry is learned and synced to the VSX peer, followed by a client IP change at the end host, the old ARP entries do not age out on the switch.
workaround: Manually clear the ARP entries on the respective VSX peer switch.

description: Symptom: When trying to snmpset VlanStaticName object, customer will see the vlan name briefly change and then change back to the old name. The name is also never updated in DB or CLI. Scenario: When trying to snmpset dot1q/ieee VlanStaticName. Workaround: Change vlan name via CLI instead of through snmpset.

description: Symptom: Kernel panic occurred while collecting support files at switch. Scenario: When multicast traffic passing through the switch. The logs piled up over a period of time, which can result in an increased system memory consumption. Workaround: Disable or enable PIM, or restart PIM daemon.

description: Symptom: Windows virtual machines detect duplicate IP addresses during Windows cluster failover. Scenario: During a Windows cluster failover, a Windows virtual machine sends an ARP probe for its own IP address to detect any duplicate IPs in the network. With ARP suppression enabled, the switch replies to the ARP probe instead of forwarding it through the VXLAN fabric. Workaround: Disable ARP suppression under the EVPN configuration.

workaround: Disable ARP suppression under the EVPN configuration.

description: Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.
symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX.

workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

description: Symptom: Incorrect sysDescr displayed in snmpwalk/get output. Scenario: When a non-default system description is configured and then reverted to the default, performing an SNMP walk or get operation may display the wrong sysDescr. Workaround: The System Description can still be viewed using the CLI.

description: Symptom: When trying to snmpset VlanStaticName object, customer will see the vlan name briefly change and then change back to the old name. The name is also never updated in DB or CLI. Scenario: When trying to snmpset dot1q/ieee VlanStaticName. Workaround: Change vlan name via CLI instead of through snmpset.

description: Symptom: Kernel panic occurred while collecting support files at switch. Scenario: When multicast traffic passing through the switch. The logs piled up over a period of time, which can result in an increased system memory consumption. Workaround: Disable or enable PIM, or restart PIM daemon.
